hypothesis: The best model so far uses tweedie_variance_power=1.5 (WRMSSE=0.94488, exp_0033). Hypothesis: tweedie_variance_power=1.25 may better fit the sales distribution by applying less variance scaling. This sits between Gaussian (power=0) and Poisson (power=1) in terms of distribution shape, potentially better matching the actual sales data characteristics. All other settings match best run (exp_0033): num_leaves=256, max_depth=8, learning_rate=0.04, n_estimators=800.
